examples.title: Algorithm: Effective Address Calculation for Addressing Modes
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating how to calculate effective addresses for different addressing modes including Immediate, Register, Direct, Indirect, Register Indirect, Indexed, Base Register, and Relative addressing
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CalculateEffectiveAddress
INPUT: instruction, registers, memory, PC
OUTPUT: effective_address, operand_value

BEGIN
 CASE OF addressing_mode:
 
 WHEN "IMMEDIATE" THEN
 // Operand is embedded in the instruction
 effective_address <- "N/A (no address)"
 operand_value <- instruction.immediate_value
 RETURN operand_value
 
 WHEN "REGISTER" THEN
 // Operand is in the specified register
 effective_address <- "N/A (register)"
 operand_value <- registers[instruction.register]
 RETURN operand_value
 
 WHEN "DIRECT" THEN
 // Instruction contains actual memory address
 effective_address <- instruction.memory_address
 operand_value <- memory[effective_address]
 RETURN operand_value
 
 WHEN "INDIRECT" THEN
 // Memory location contains address of operand
 address_ptr <- instruction.memory_address
 effective_address <- memory[address_ptr]
 operand_value <- memory[effective_address]
 RETURN operand_value
 
 WHEN "REGISTER_INDIRECT" THEN
 // Register contains address of operand
 effective_address <- registers[instruction.register]
 operand_value <- memory[effective_address]
 RETURN operand_value
 
 WHEN "INDEXED" THEN
 // EA = Base Address + Index Register Value
 effective_address <- instruction.base_address + registers[instruction.index_register]
 operand_value <- memory[effective_address]
 RETURN operand_value
 
 WHEN "BASE_REGISTER" THEN
 // EA = Base Register + Offset
 effective_address <- registers[instruction.base_register] + instruction.offset
 operand_value <- memory[effective_address]
 RETURN operand_value
 
 WHEN "RELATIVE" THEN
 // EA = PC + Displacement
 effective_address <- PC + instruction.displacement
 operand_value <- memory[effective_address]
 RETURN operand_value
 
 WHEN "IMPLIED" THEN
 // No address calculation needed
 effective_address <- "N/A (implied)"
 operand_value <- "Operation specific"
 RETURN operand_value
 
 END CASE
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows how the CPU calculates effective addresses for each type of addressing mode. For Immediate mode, no address is needed - the value is in the instruction itself. For Register mode, the operand is in a CPU register. Direct addressing uses the address from the instruction. Indirect requires two memory reads. Register Indirect uses a register to hold the address. Indexed adds base and index. Relative uses PC + displacement.

examples.title: Python: Addressing Modes Simulator
examples.description: Complete Python implementation demonstrating all addressing modes with a CPU simulator class that shows memory access patterns and effective address calculations
examples.code:
"""
Addressing Modes Simulator - Python Implementation
Demonstrates all major addressing modes used in computer architecture
"""

class AddressingModesSimulator:
 def __init__(self):
 # Initialize memory (simple 256-byte array)
 self.memory = [0] * 256
 # Initialize registers (8 general-purpose registers)
 self.registers = [0] * 8
 # Program Counter
 self.pc = 0
 # Initialize some test data
 self._initialize_test_data()
 
 def _initialize_test_data(self):
 """Set up test data in memory for demonstration"""
 # Direct address: memory[100] = 42
 self.memory[100] = 42
 # Indirect: memory[200] contains address 100
 self.memory[200] = 100
 # Indirect: memory[200] -> points to 100 -> value 42
 # Array example at base address 50
 self.memory[50] = 10
 self.memory[51] = 20
 self.memory[52] = 30
 self.memory[53] = 40
 self.memory[54] = 50
 
 def immediate_addressing(self, value):
 """
 IMMEDIATE: Operand is embedded in the instruction
 No memory access needed for the operand
 Example: MOV R1, #25
 """
 print(f" Immediate Addressing: Value = {value}")
 return value
 
 def register_addressing(self, reg_num):
 """
 REGISTER: Operand is in the specified register
 No memory access needed
 Example: ADD R1, R2
 """
 value = self.registers[reg_num]
 print(f" Register Addressing: R{reg_num} = {value}")
 return value
 
 def direct_addressing(self, address):
 """
 DIRECT (Absolute): Instruction contains actual memory address
 One memory access to get operand
 Example: LOAD R1, 100
 """
 print(f" Direct Addressing: Address = {address}")
 value = self.memory[address]
 print(f" Memory[{address}] = {value}")
 return value
 
 def indirect_addressing(self, address_ptr):
 """
 INDIRECT: Memory location contains address of operand
 Two memory accesses: first to get address, then to get operand
 Example: LOAD R1, (200) - if memory[200]=100, loads from memory[100]
 """
 print(f" Indirect Addressing: Pointer Address = {address_ptr}")
 effective_address = self.memory[address_ptr]
 print(f" Pointer points to address: {effective_address}")
 value = self.memory[effective_address]
 print(f" Memory[{effective_address}] = {value}")
 return value
 
 def register_indirect_addressing(self, reg_num):
 """
 REGISTER INDIRECT: Register contains address of operand
 One memory access using address from register
 Example: LOAD R1, (R2) - if R2=100, loads from memory[100]
 """
 address = self.registers[reg_num]
 print(f" Register Indirect: R{reg_num} contains address {address}")
 value = self.memory[address]
 print(f" Memory[{address}] = {value}")
 return value
 
 def indexed_addressing(self, base_addr, index_reg):
 """
 INDEXED: Effective address = Base + Index Register
 One memory access after address calculation
 Example: LOAD R1, 50(R2) - if R2=2, loads from memory[52]
 """
 index_value = self.registers[index_reg]
 effective_address = base_addr + index_value
 print(f" Indexed Addressing: Base={base_addr}, Index(R{index_reg})={index_value}")
 print(f" Effective Address = {base_addr} + {index_value} = {effective_address}")
 value = self.memory[effective_address]
 print(f" Memory[{effective_address}] = {value}")
 return value
 
 def base_register_addressing(self, base_reg, offset):
 """
 BASE REGISTER: Effective address = Base Register + Offset
 Used for segmentation and position-independent code
 Example: LOAD R1, 10(R2) - if R2=50, loads from memory[60]
 """
 base_value = self.registers[base_reg]
 effective_address = base_value + offset
 print(f" Base Register: Base(R{base_reg})={base_value}, Offset={offset}")
 print(f" Effective Address = {base_value} + {offset} = {effective_address}")
 value = self.memory[effective_address]
 print(f" Memory[{effective_address}] = {value}")
 return value
 
 def relative_addressing(self, displacement):
 """
 RELATIVE: Effective address = PC + Displacement
 Used for branches and position-independent code
 Example: JUMP +10
 """
 current_pc = self.pc
 effective_address = current_pc + displacement
 print(f" Relative Addressing: PC={current_pc}, Displacement={displacement}")
 print(f" Effective Address = {current_pc} + {displacement} = {effective_address}")
 return effective_address
 
 def implied_addressing(self):
 """
 IMPLIED: Operands are implicit (no explicit operand)
 No address calculation needed
 Example: CLC (Clear Carry), NOP
 """
 print(f" Implied Addressing: No operand specified")
 print(f" Operation affects processor flags/state directly")
 return "Operation Complete"


def demonstrate_addressing_modes():
 """Demonstrate all addressing modes with the simulator"""
 sim = AddressingModesSimulator()
 
 print("=" * 60)
 print("ADDRESSING MODES DEMONSTRATION")
 print("=" * 60)
 
 # Setup registers
 sim.registers[2] = 2 # Index register for indexed addressing
 sim.registers[3] = 50 # Base register for base-register addressing
 
 # 1. Immediate Addressing
 print("\n1. IMMEDIATE ADDRESSING")
 print(" Instruction: MOV R1, #25")
 result = sim.immediate_addressing(25)
 print(f" Result: {result} (operand directly from instruction)")
 
 # 2. Register Addressing
 print("\n2. REGISTER ADDRESSING")
 print(" Instruction: ADD R1, R2")
 sim.registers[2] = 15
 result = sim.register_addressing(2)
 print(f" Result: {result} (value from register)")
 
 # 3. Direct Addressing
 print("\n3. DIRECT ADDRESSING")
 print(" Instruction: LOAD R1, 100")
 result = sim.direct_addressing(100)
 print(f" Result: {result} (1 memory access)")
 
 # 4. Indirect Addressing
 print("\n4. INDIRECT ADDRESSING")
 print(" Instruction: LOAD R1, (200)")
 print(" Memory[200] = 100, Memory[100] = 42")
 result = sim.indirect_addressing(200)
 print(f" Result: {result} (2 memory accesses)")
 
 # 5. Register Indirect Addressing
 print("\n5. REGISTER INDIRECT ADDRESSING")
 print(" Instruction: LOAD R1, (R2)")
 sim.registers[2] = 100
 result = sim.register_indirect_addressing(2)
 print(f" Result: {result} (1 memory access using register)")
 
 # 6. Indexed Addressing
 print("\n6. INDEXED ADDRESSING")
 print(" Instruction: LOAD R1, 50(R2)")
 print(" Base=50, Index(R2)=2")
 result = sim.indexed_addressing(50, 2)
 print(f" Result: {result} (array element access)")
 
 # 7. Base Register Addressing
 print("\n7. BASE REGISTER ADDRESSING")
 print(" Instruction: LOAD R1, 0(R3)")
 print(" Base(R3)=50, Offset=0")
 result = sim.base_register_addressing(3, 0)
 print(f" Result: {result}")
 
 # 8. Relative Addressing
 print("\n8. RELATIVE ADDRESSING")
 print(" Instruction: JUMP +25")
 sim.pc = 100
 result = sim.relative_addressing(25)
 print(f" Result: Jump to address {result}")
 
 # 9. Implied Addressing
 print("\n9. IMPLIED ADDRESSING")
 print(" Instruction: CLC (Clear Carry Flag)")
 result = sim.implied_addressing()
 print(f" Result: {result}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
 demonstrate_addressing_modes()
examples.explanation: This Python implementation creates a complete simulator for all addressing modes. The AddressingModesSimulator class demonstrates Immediate (value in instruction), Register (value in CPU register), Direct (address in instruction), Indirect (pointer to address), Register Indirect (register holds address), Indexed (base+index), Base Register (base+offset), Relative (PC+displacement), and Implied (no operand) addressing. Each method shows memory access patterns and effective address calculations.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for most modes; O(2) for indirect (two memory accesses)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for memory array, O(1) for registers

examples.title: C: Addressing Modes Implementation
examples.description: C program demonstrating all addressing modes with a simple CPU simulation showing memory operations and effective address calculations
examples.explanation: This C implementation demonstrates all addressing modes with a complete CPU simulation. The program includes structures for CPU state (registers, memory, PC), functions for each addressing mode showing effective address calculations, and clear output showing memory access patterns. Immediate and Register modes show zero memory accesses, Direct shows one, Indirect shows two, and others show address calculations based on base+index or PC+displacement.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for most modes; O(n) where n is number of memory accesses (1-2)
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for memory array O(1) for registers
